title: Why exactly
wikitext:
[]: Why, exactly?             Quote Post by GoatMan » Wed Feb 04, 2009 2:19 am Okay, you've got three kids (yes, KIDS, not mature, experienced soldiers, but children, as the human brain doesn't fully mature until age 25-26), each of which should at least grasp the concept that what is being done to them is wrong, and they are given unlimited access to the world's ultimate weapons system. Why, exactly, didn't at least one of these pilots go nuts, and revolt against "lawful authority"? From my perspective, the first thing I would have done as a Child in the Eva-verse was figure would be to figure out the logistics to turn mankind's ultimate weapon against them. I mean, as much as the whole Cain/Abel "Am I my brother's keeper?" question has to ethically be answered "yes", when the entire population of telephone sanitizers can't build the wheel because they can't decide what color it is, isn't the solution a child like Shinji, Asuka, or Rei would devise end up being "humanity needs a good ol' fashioned purging?" Granted, I get the whole Japanese culture thing of how one's worth is determined by their usefulness to the group (hence, discrimination against those who are physically handicapped, the non-existence of retirement, and the running theme in most mecha anime of "doing the job because you're strong, and everyone else is weak"). However, if you have a bunch of corrupt cowards who force a child, supposedly a symbol of innocence along with the hope for the future (which they are supposed to be teaching the values humanity holds for the next generation) to fight a war they started, you'd think the kid would get the hint, and say "you don't deserve survival." So again, why does Shinji decide mankind is worth saving? All anyone did when the truth was revealed to them was shrug it off, and go about their own business (Kaji just wanted the truth, not to do anything about it, but to understand objectively what was going on, and Misato just decided to keep on fighting Angels when Kaji showed her Lillith, among the other secrets of NERV). I mean seriously, if you're told the entire world you've struggled to protect thus far is shown to be throwing away all you've worked for, do you just go back to filing your taxes and holding your 9-5 job like a good little citizen? I demand satisfaction in the form of a Sergeant Todd [/url]

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Wed Feb 04, 2009 3:44 am I don't think Shinji felt that the world was worth saving. I think it was the people he knew that he felt close to that he wanted to protect and be acknowledged by. Remember when he killed Kaworu and left Asuka's hospital room in EoE? He had hit rock bottom and was prepared to die because the things that mattered to him (toji, kensuke, Rei, Asuka, Misato, etc.) were lost. He was alienated from Misato and Rei was no longer the person he knew. Shinji and the other pilots were being used, but they gained important things even as tools by piloting the Eva ( sense of identity, self-worth, praise, meaning) and to them, these things were worth being had by exploitation, though I don't think Asuka felt exploited. Even when Rei's relationship with Gendo failed I still think she felt a tie to the Eva and so she had no qualms about piloting. I know everyone hates Sadamoto's manga but Shinji said something very interesting after he killed Kaworu and Asuka trying to strangle him. He said something like 'Is this world even worth protecting?' since he had lost everything he piloted for. So: If you piloted I don't think you'd care for the world ( so many people you don't know, aren't attached to, and beyond your experience) so much as the people you learned to be comfortable with. So pilot for them. When they are gone, you lose the will to pilot.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Feb 04, 2009 4:47 am Episode 19 wrote: Shinji (RADIO - OFF): Don't make me more angry by saying things like that. Unit 01 has 185 seconds of power left in its reserves. That's enough to destroy at least half of Headquarters. AOBA: The way he is right now, he'll do it too. IBUKI: Shinji, listen to us! If Commander Ikari hadn't made that decision, everyone could have been killed! Shinji: I said that doesn't matter! My father... That bastard tried to kill Toji! With my hands... Shinji (RADIO - OFF): Father, you're there, aren't you? Say something! Answer me! IKARI: Increase the pressure of the LCL to maximum. We have no time to waste on a childish tantrum. Shinji: I've still got a direct control circuit... Damn! Damn! Damn...

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Wed Feb 04, 2009 4:56 am I don't think it really mattered. They were screwed either way.

[]:             Quote Post by GoatMan » Wed Feb 04, 2009 11:05 am Ornette wrote: Episode 19 wrote: Shinji (RADIO - OFF): Don't make me more angry by saying things like that. Unit 01 has 185 seconds of power left in its reserves. That's enough to destroy at least half of Headquarters. AOBA: The way he is right now, he'll do it too. IBUKI: Shinji, listen to us! If Commander Ikari hadn't made that decision, everyone could have been killed! Shinji: I said that doesn't matter! My father... That bastard tried to kill Toji! With my hands... Shinji (RADIO - OFF): Father, you're there, aren't you? Say something! Answer me! IKARI: Increase the pressure of the LCL to maximum. We have no time to waste on a childish tantrum. Shinji: I've still got a direct control circuit... Damn! Damn! Damn...

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Wed Feb 04, 2009 12:35 pm Shinji doesn't seem the type to kill an entire headquarters full of people (even in ep19 I didn't believe he'd actually DO it). Look how much killing Kaworu fucked him up. Asuka's doing it for attention/glory so she isn't gonna blow up NERV. And Rei's doing it for Gendo. Why would they turn on their masters? If Kensuke was a pilot he'd probably go fuck-nuts and kill everyone, after all it's always the nerdy ones you gotta watch.

[]: Re: Why, exactly?             Quote Post by VoidEater » Wed Feb 04, 2009 12:38 pm GoatMan wrote: Okay, you've got three kids (yes, KIDS, not mature, experienced soldiers, but children, as the human brain doesn't fully mature until age 25-26), each of which should at least grasp the concept that what is being done to them is wrong, and they are given unlimited access to the world's ultimate weapons system.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Feb 04, 2009 2:00 pm GoatMan wrote: Yeah, but think about the scene with the 14th Angel attack. Rei got her hands on an N2 mine right under everyone's noses without raising suspicion. Who's to say Shinji couldn't have simply played a game of "catch" with said N2 mine, and headquarters loses?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Feb 04, 2009 2:55 pm Better question: Why the hell would they just randomly decide to kill people in massive purgings like that? Shinji is so ethical that he grows suicidal killing something that LOOKED human, and totally flipped off his gourd when he INJURED someone else. Rei doesn't have much of a motive one way or another, but it's kind've against her Prime Directive to kill people, given that she's our Mommy. And Asuka? If she kills people, she's not going to get her precious ego stroking.

[]:             Quote Post by GoatMan » Thu Feb 05, 2009 2:06 am AuraTwilight wrote: Better question: Why the hell would they just randomly decide to kill people in massive purgings like that? Shinji is so ethical that he grows suicidal killing something that LOOKED human, and totally flipped off his gourd when he INJURED someone else. Rei doesn't have much of a motive one way or another, but it's kind've against her Prime Directive to kill people, given that she's our Mommy. And Asuka? If she kills people, she's not going to get her precious ego stroking.

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Thu Feb 05, 2009 11:46 am She did eventually, just too little too late...

[]:             Quote Post by Annihilationscape » Wed Feb 11, 2009 2:51 pm My take is, Shinji is too concerned with not killing anyone to be pushed to that point; this is simply a character trait written into his character to prevent the scenario you describe from happening. Another child might have done so. Rei wouldn't kill. She won't even eat meat. Besides that, Rei II practically worships Gendo, so she would not have a reason to rebel as such. Asuka absorbs praise from others, so that's one othe reasons she doesn't rebel against NERV. Also, she was trained from a very early age, so in effect she was conditioned to be a good little soldier.

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Wed Feb 11, 2009 4:18 pm Why not? The needs of the many, outweigh the needs of the few.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Wed Feb 11, 2009 7:38 pm Dartz wrote: The needs of the many, outweigh the needs of the few.
